topics.name: Atomic Structure, Periodic Table, and Bonding
topics.description: Subatomic particles, isotopes, electron configuration, periodic trends, electrovalent, covalent, metallic, and hydrogen bonding.

subtopics.name: Atomic Structure and Isotopy
objectives: Identify protons, neutrons, electrons, atomic number Z, and mass number A
Write s, p, d electron configurations for elements 1 to 30
Calculate relative atomic mass RAM from isotopic abundances

subtopics.name: Periodic Table and Periodic Trends
objectives: Classify elements into Groups I-VIII and Periods 1-4
Explain periodic trends: atomic radius, ionization energy, electronegativity, electron affinity

subtopics.name: Chemical Bonding
objectives: Compare electrovalent (ionic), covalent, co-ordinate (dative), metallic, and hydrogen bonds
Relate bond type to physical properties (melting point, solubility, electrical conductivity)

topics.name: Stoichiometry, Gas Laws, and Solutions
topics.description: Mole concept, chemical equations, gas laws, solutions, solubility, and volumetric analysis.

subtopics.name: Mole Concept and Chemical Equations
objectives: Calculate molar mass, empirical formula, molecular formula, and percentage composition
Balance chemical equations and perform stoichiometric calculations

subtopics.name: Gas Laws and Kinetic Theory
objectives: Apply Boyle's Law, Charles' Law, Graham's Law of Diffusion, and Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures
Calculate gas volume at standard temperature and pressure (STP = 22.4 dm³/mol)

subtopics.name: Solubility and Volumetric Analysis
objectives: Define solute, solvent, saturated, unsaturated, and supersaturated solutions
Plot and interpret solubility curves
Perform acid-base titration calculations (molarity, concentration in g/dm³, purity)

topics.name: Acids, Bases, Salts, and Electrochemistry
topics.description: Acids, bases, pH scale, salt preparation, oxidation-reduction (redox) reactions, and electrolysis.

subtopics.name: Acids, Bases, and Salts
objectives: Compare Arrhenius, Bronsted-Lowry, and Lewis concepts of acids and bases
Calculate pH = -log[H+] and pOH of solutions
Classify salts (normal, acid, basic, double, complex) and describe preparation methods

subtopics.name: Redox Reactions and Electrochemistry
objectives: Assign oxidation numbers and identify oxidizing and reducing agents
Balance redox equations using half-reaction method
State Faraday's 1st and 2nd Laws of Electrolysis and calculate mass deposited m = Q M / (z F)

topics.name: Organic Chemistry and Macromolecules
topics.description: Hydrocarbons, alkanols, alkanoic acids, esters, fats, oils, polymers, and synthetic materials.

subtopics.name: Hydrocarbons: Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes, and Benzene
objectives: Name organic compounds using IUPAC nomenclature
Describe fractional distillation of crude oil and cracking of petroleum fractions
Compare structural isomerism and chemical reactions of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and benzene

subtopics.name: Alkanols, Alkanoic Acids, and Esters
objectives: Describe preparation, properties, and reactions of primary, secondary, and tertiary alkanols
Explain esterification reaction and saponification of fats and oils to produce soap

subtopics.name: Polymers and Synthetic Materials
objectives: Distinguish addition polymerization (polyethene, PVC) and condensation polymerization (nylon, terylene)
Describe structure and properties of carbohydrates, proteins, and synthetic polymers
